public_functions.behavior_description: Initialize GTM for 3-phase complementary PWM on a TOM cluster using a TOM timer as the base and a PwmHl helper for paired outputs. Steps: 1) Enable the GTM module and enable the FXCLK clock domain used by TOM. 2) Initialize a TOM timer configuration with defaults, then set the PWM base frequency (20 kHz), select FXCLK0 as the clock source, choose the TOM cluster and a timer channel to act as the base counter, and initialize the timer driver handle. 3) Route six TOM output pins (three complementary pairs) to the pad controller using the generic TOM TOUT pin map function, configuring push-pull mode and the requested pad driver; enable both high-side and low-side outputs for each pair as active-high. 4) Initialize a TOM PwmHl configuration: set three channel pairs, set dead-time (0.5 us) and minimum pulse (1.0 us), set output mode to push-pull and active state to high for both sides, bind it to the initialized TOM timer/base. Initialize the PwmHl driver, then set operating mode to center-aligned. 5) Update the TOM timer's input frequency from GTM CMU to ensure correct period resolution, then start the timer running. 6) Compute initial on-times for the three phases as 25%, 50%, and 75% of the current PWM period and store them in the module's persistent state array. 7) Perform a synchronous shadow transfer sequence: disable update on the timer, write the three on-time values through the PwmHl set-on-time API (which applies complementary behavior with hardware-enforced dead-time/minPulse), then apply the update on the timer to latch all new compare values at the next period boundary. The function does not perform any scheduling or delays; it only configures hardware and initializes the persistent PWM state used by the runtime updater.

Update three phase on-times in a cyclic ramp and apply them synchronously at the PWM period boundary. Steps: 1) Read the current PWM period from the module state (via the associated timer/driver) and compute a duty increment step as a fixed fraction of the period. Also compute duty limits corresponding to the configured minimum pulse width (1.0 us) and a maximum threshold slightly below full-scale to respect minPulse both at the beginning and end of the pulse window. 2) For each of the three phases, add the step to the stored on-time value; if the result exceeds the configured maximum threshold, wrap back to the minimum threshold to keep the duty within allowed bounds. 3) Perform a synchronous update using the TOM TGC shadow-transfer sequence: disable update on the timer, write the three on-time values via the PwmHl set-on-time API (hardware applies complementary timing and enforces the configured dead-time/minPulse), then apply the timer update to latch all channels at the next period boundary. No timing or delays are implemented here; this function assumes it is called periodically by the main loop.

header_selection.reconciliation_reason: LLM driver_calls use IfxGtm_Tom_* API, not IfxGtm_Pwm (the reference API). Updated to match actual usage.
